reviewer_notes: Manual review checked whether the response evidence span supports the mechanical clean bucket and whether the primary exemption is defensible as a single-label gold answer. Review did not inspect system predictions.

rows.rationale: CFPB states responsive supervision records would be withheld in full under 5 U.S.C. 552(b)(8).
CFPB states responsive horizontal review records would be withheld in full under 5 U.S.C. 552(b)(8).
FAA says names in the FOIA log were redacted and withheld under Exemption 6.
The response describes a recommendation to an Initial Denial Authority and says a final decision is still pending, so this is not a clean final application of Exemption 4.
DOE identifies responsive documents and withholds information under Exemption 5 with deliberative-process explanation.
NASA states three pages are withheld in full pursuant to FOIA Exemption 5.
DOJ appeal response says information was properly withheld in full under Exemption 5.
The response cites both Exemption 5 and Exemption 6, with Exemption 5 first. The mechanical primary of b6 is not a defensible single-label clean gold.
FDIC referral response withholds the responsive pages in entirety under Exemption 8.
FinCEN invokes Bank Secrecy Act protections and FOIA Exemption 3 for SAR records.
FinCEN invokes Bank Secrecy Act protections and FOIA Exemption 3 for SAR records.
FinCEN invokes Bank Secrecy Act protections and FOIA Exemption 3 for SAR records.
ATF identifies eTrace/firearms-trace data and states it is exempt under Exemption 3 and a separate statute.
FBI gives a Glomar response tied to a pending investigation and invokes Exemption 7(A).
FinCEN invokes Bank Secrecy Act protections and FOIA Exemption 3 for SAR-related records.
IRS denies third-party return information under Section 6103 and FOIA Exemption 3.
The response says USTRANSCOM routinely applies Exemption 6 but may apply additional exemptions; it does not cleanly apply B6 to located responsive records.
FCC says personal identifying information in responsive complaints was redacted under Exemption 6.
NIST email says a responsive document is being partially redacted pursuant to FOIA Exemption B6.
Army AvMC states responsive records contain information exempt under 5 U.S.C. 552(b)(6).
Department of Labor denies access to personal addresses, phone numbers, and personal email addresses under Exemption 6.
FTC gives a Glomar response because confirming records would reveal information exempt under Exemption 7(A).
TVA says personal information in an enclosed responsive record was redacted under Exemption 6.
FCC says one responsive record is withheld in full under Exemption 5 and explains deliberative-process privilege.
DOE says responsive documents are released with Exemption 6 redactions protecting personal privacy.
